meaning: n.最高度adj.<口>最高质量的,第一流的
examples: At Howard University law School, Houston trained a cod-ray of topnotch black lawyers.
This is often your first introduction to a new employer or boss — so make sure it’s topnotch.
John C Reilly also turns in topnotch work as the schmuk husband Amos.
The marriages and write-offs by Japanese institutions highlight a key plank in the strategies of topnotch Asian banks: Beef up your capital.
